description: public university in the United States
description_wiki: The University of Kentucky (UK, UKY, or U of K) is a public land-grant research university in Lexington, Kentucky, United States.

description: city in Boone County, Kentucky, United States
description_wiki: Union is a home-rule-class city in Boone County, Kentucky, United States.

description: city in Ohio, United States; county seat of Hamilton County
description_wiki: Cincinnati (  SIN-sih-NAT-ee; colloquially nicknamed Cincy) is the most populous city in Hamilton County, Ohio, United States, and its county seat.
Grant County is a county located in the northern part of the U.S. commonwealth of Kentucky.

description: network of youth organizations in the United States
description_wiki: 4-H is a national youth development program administered by the Cooperative Extension System under the National Institute of Food and Agriculture (NIFA) and United States Department of Agriculture (USDA).
Greta Tintin Eleonora Ernman Thunberg (Swedish: [ˈɡrêːta ˈtʉ̂ːnbærj] ; born 3 January 2003) is a Swedish activist known for pressuring governments to address climate change and social issues.

entity: No One Is Too Small to Make a Difference
surface_forms: No One Is Too Small to Make a Difference
description_wiki: No One Is Too Small to Make a Difference is a book by climate activist Greta Thunberg.

The global COVID-19 pandemic (also known as the coronavirus pandemic), caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2), began with an outbreak in Wuhan, China, in December 2019.

description: motorsport track in the United States
description_wiki: Kentucky Speedway is an inactive 1.500-mile (2.414 km) tri-oval intermediate speedway in Sparta, Kentucky.
Andrew Graham Beshear ( bə-SHEER; born November 29, 1977) is an American politician and attorney serving since 2019 as the 63rd governor of Kentucky.
